title: Service Layer — Book API
description:
### Your Task

Build a Book CRUD API with a proper 3-layer architecture: Controller → Service → Repository.

### Why Service Layer?

In real projects and assessments, the controller should NEVER call the repository directly. The service layer contains business logic and acts as a bridge.

### Requirements

- `Book` entity: `id` (Long), `title` (String), `author` (String), `genre` (String), `price` (Double), `inStock` (Boolean)
- `BookRepository` extending `JpaRepository`
- `BookService` with all CRUD methods (the controller calls this, NOT the repo)
- `BookController` at `/api/books`

### Endpoints

- `GET /api/books` → Return all books (200)
- `GET /api/books/{id}` → Return book by ID (200 or 404)
- `POST /api/books` → Create book (201)
- `PUT /api/books/{id}` → Update book (200 or 404)
- `DELETE /api/books/{id}` → Delete book (204 or 404)

### Key Concepts

- `@Service` annotation on the service class
- Controller injects the Service, NOT the Repository
- Service injects the Repository
- Same CRUD, just properly layered
hints: Inject BookRepository into BookService via constructor
Inject BookService into BookController via constructor
The controller should NEVER have a reference to the repository
